---
title: "Exam"
subtitle: "Thursday, June 11, 2026"
---

There is one exam, given on **Thursday, June 11**. It has two parts, each worth half of the exam grade.

## In-class (20% of course grade)

- During class time (9:35–10:50 a.m.).
- **On paper, closed book, no AI tools.**
- Covers material through end of Week 3 plus Week 4 days 1–2 (joins, importing).
- Mix of: short answer; "predict the output"; "write a small chunk of code"; "find the bug"; reading a plot or summary table.

## What to study

Probably the single best preparation is to **redo old homework, activities, and quizzes from scratch without looking at your prior answers**.

- All `dplyr` verbs we've covered.
- Reading and writing `ggplot2` code.
- Identifying common bugs (`==` vs `=`, factor vs character, missing argument names).
- Reading provenance and structure of a tibble from a `glimpse()` printout.
- Joins: who keeps which rows, what NAs mean in the output.

## Materials

- TODO: practice problems
- TODO: in-class exam paper version
- TODO: take-home QMD with starter data
- TODO: solutions (post-exam)
